title: The Tech Sector and In-House IT
summary: Key actors in the tech sector, the evolution from Web 1.0 to Web 3.0, social media in business, and the role of in-house IT departments.
content_html:
The Tech Sector and In-House IT

The tech sector is a complex ecosystem with distinct players who interact with and increasingly influence all of society and business.

Key Players in the Tech Sector

  • Start-ups: Innovative, fast-moving companies disrupting existing markets.
  • Big Tech: Consolidated technology giants (e.g. FAANG).
  • The Consultants: Large IT consulting firms helping businesses deploy technology.
  • In-House IT: Internal IT staff and systems within an organisation.
  • The Rest of the Business World: Organisations adopting and using technology.

Evolution of the Web

  • Web 1.0: Static pages, no user-generated content. Read-only.
  • Web 2.0: Collaboration, sharing, user participation, social media, mobility, multimedia. The web as a platform.
  • Semantic Web (Web 3.0): Links web content in a way meaningful to machines -- transitioning from a web of documents to a web of data.

Social Media in Business

Social media: Websites that rely on user participation and user-contributed content.

Social network: An application connecting people by matching profile information.

Business adopted social media because customers use it to find and evaluate local businesses, and it provides rich data analytics on customer behaviour.

In-House IT

In-House IT refers to the IT staff, processes, and computers located inside a business.

Advantages: Always available, knows the business and its IT systems, can build tailored solutions.

Disadvantages: Employees are costly, skills need ongoing updating, technology needs ongoing refreshing, risk of becoming disconnected from industry trends.

Information Richness and Reach

The web transformed business through information richness (interactivity, multiple cues, language varieties, socio-emotional cues) and reach (global audience accessible from anywhere). These two factors combined create unprecedented communication and commerce possibilities.

exercises.title: Web 2.0 characteristic
exercises.prompt: Which is a defining characteristic of Web 2.0?

exercises.title: In-house IT advantage
exercises.prompt: A key advantage of in-house IT over outsourced IT is:
options: They know the business and its systems intimately
exercises.answer: They know the business and its systems intimately

exercises.title: Semantic Web
exercises.prompt: The Semantic Web (Web 3.0) transitions the web from:
options: A web of documents to a web of data
exercises.answer: A web of documents to a web of data

exercises.title: Social media definition
exercises.prompt: Social media is best defined as:
options: Websites that rely on user participation and user-contributed content
exercises.answer: Websites that rely on user participation and user-contributed content

exercises.title: Tech sector players
exercises.prompt: Which is NOT one of the main player categories in the tech sector?

exercises.title: In-house IT disadvantage
exercises.prompt: A key disadvantage of maintaining in-house IT is:
options: They lack knowledge of the business
High employee costs and ongoing need to update skills and technology
They are not available when needed
exercises.answer: High employee costs and ongoing need to update skills and technology

exercises.title: Web 1.0 characteristic
options: Static pages and lack of user-generated content
exercises.answer: Static pages and lack of user-generated content

exercises.title: Business social media adoption
exercises.prompt: A primary reason businesses adopted social media was:
options: Customers use it to find and evaluate local businesses, and it provides rich data analytics
exercises.answer: Customers use it to find and evaluate local businesses, and it provides rich data analytics
